$comment: Recursive is a Go int that also accepts a boolean in the wire format: true means infinite recursion (-1) and false means no recursion (0). It always marshals back out as an integer.
title: Recursive
description:
Recursive controls whether component references are transferred along with
their parent component, and how deep that recursion goes.

anyOf.description: Recursion depth: -1 means infinite recursion, 0 means no recursion, n > 0 limits recursion to n levels.
Shorthand: true maps to infinite recursion (-1), false to no recursion (0).
